channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: San Francisco Mayor-elect Taps OpenAI CEO to Lead Transition Team
item.description: San Francisco mayor-elect Daniel Lurie selects OpenAI CEO Sam Altman as a key advisor, aiming to revamp the city's relationships with tech leaders.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Zero Trust Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2024
item.description: Explore the evolution of zero trust in government The post Federal Executive Forum Zero Trust Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: 32 state attorneys general call on Congress to pass Kids Online Safety Act
item.description: A bipartisan coalition of more than 30 attorney generals is urging Congress to pass the Kids Online Safety Act (KOSA) this year, stating the legislation will “establish better safeguards for minors online.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Amazon and Elon Musk's SpaceX challenge labor agency's constitutionality in federal court
item.description: Attorneys for Amazon and Elon Musk’s SpaceX have argued in a federal appeals court that the National Labor Relations Board’s structure is unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A New AI-Powered Platform: Aggie Aims to Revolutionize Social Media Management for Businesses
item.description: Discover the innovative tool Aggie, which leverages AI to create a steady stream of marketing content for social media platforms, making it easier for businesses to manage their online presence.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational
item.description: The ETAC has quickly scaled from a pilot project to an operational center at the Energy Department for analyzing cyber threats like Volt Typhoon. The post Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawrence Livermore supercomputer is crowned world’s speediest
item.description: "El Capitan" takes top spot in global supercomputer race, helping tackle daunting challenges in national security and science.
item.dc:creator: Lisa M. Krieger, The Mercury News

item.title: Helpful hack: Customize your home screen by adding widgets on your iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows you how to add, customize and manage widgets for quick access to apps and info from your iPhone home screen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mainframe Modernization: Kyndryl and Microsoft Expand Partnership
item.description: Kyndryl and Microsoft expand partnership to include mainframe modernization, security services, and cloud migration offerings.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The ‘Great IT Rebrand’: Restructuring IT for business success
item.description: Traditionally viewed as rock-solid and steady, the insurance industry is not exactly associated with taking big risks.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump names Brendan Carr, senior GOP leader at FCC, to lead the agency
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has named the senior Republican on the Federal Communications Commission as the new chairman of the agency tasked with regulating broadcasting, telecommunications and broadband.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The worst passwords you could have in 2024 — or any year
item.description: Don't rely on easy-to-remember passwords too much — because all of us tend to think more alike than we'd think.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, NewsNation

item.title: How a wrong Google search can compromise your data and bring law enforcement calling
item.description: Wrong Google searches can not only compromise your device and personal data. They can also bring law enforcement to your doorstep. Hackers are targeting Google searches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s new DOGE task force is tailor-made to benefit Elon Musk’s businesses
item.description: On Tuesday, President-elect Donald Trump named Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy as cochairs of a new Department of Government Efficiency, or DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: Go language evolving for future hardware, AI workloads
item.description: The Go programming language having just turned 15 years old on November 10, proponents now are planning to adapt the Go language to large multicore systems, the latest vector and matrix hardware instructions, and the needs of AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The EU seeks proposals for AI that should be banned
item.description: The EU, which is now developing guidelines for how the region’s new AI law must be complied with, has started collecting opinions in two areas via an online survey. The first area involves how the law should define AI systems (compared to traditional software).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tech group presses Trump to review AI regulations
item.description: A tech lobbying group called on President-elect Trump and his team to ensure the U.S. maintains leadership in the development and deployment of artificial intelligence (AI) as it crafts the incoming regulations and policy on the advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Biden administration finalizes $6.6B in chips grants for TSMC before Trump takes office
item.description: The White House on Friday announced it had finalized an agreement with a major semiconductor manufacturer to invest billions of dollars in facilities in Arizona as President Biden looks to cement parts of his legislative legacy before leaving office in January.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: O2 unleashes AI grandma on scammers
item.description: Research by British telecommunications provider O2 has found that seven in ten Britons (71 percent) would like to take revenge on scammers who have tried to trick them or their loved ones. At the same time, however, one in two people does not want to waste their time on it.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Exoskeleton helps paralyzed people regain independence
item.description: The WalkON Suit F1, an exoskeleton developed to help people with disabilities, can actually walk over to a person in a wheelchair, solving a major problem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia's plan to ban children from social media proves popular and problematic
item.description: The Australian government's plan to ban children from social media to protect them from harm has won almost universal political support.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 18 states sue SEC, Gensler for 'regulatory overreach' on crypto
item.description: Eighteen Republican attorneys general sued the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and Chair Gary Gensler on Thursday for allegedly overstepping the agency’s authority in its enforcement actions against the cryptocurrency industry.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Man who stole and laundered roughly $1B in bitcoin is sentenced to 5 years in prison
item.description: A computer expert who stole bitcoin worth billions of dollars at current prices — and then spent years laundering some of the hacked cryptocurrency with help from his wife — has been sentenced to five years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Versa unveils VersaONE converged security, networking platform
item.description: Versa Networks is offering a platform that brings together its SASE, SSE, SD-WAN and SD-LAN components under one umbrella. Called VersaONE, the converged platform is aimed at reducing risk and lowering infrastructure complexity and costs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why that Google Meet error might be a sneaky hacker trick
item.description: ClickFix, a new scam targeting computer users, is on the rise in the U.S. The scam prompts its targets to click on a link to fix a problem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Agile Manifesto was ahead of its time
item.description: Last week I went on a short rant about scrum, and how it isn’t agile and is, well, dumb. It occurred to me that one of the obstacles to truly being agile has been the limits of software technology. The way we had to build and deliver software didn’t lend itself to flexibility.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Facebook and Instagram users in Europe can opt for less personalized ads
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Facebook and Instagram users in Europe will get the option to see less personalized ads if they don't want to pay for an ad-free subscription, social media company Meta said Tuesday, bowing to pressure from Brussels over privacy and digital competition concerns.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
